methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $72M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $11.0B.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Lumotive, a Series B chip and semiconductor company that has raised $72M to date, is evaluated against a comp universe of 11 peers spanning public equities (Analogic, NVIDIA, AMD) and private frontier-tech firms including Etched, SambaNova Systems, Positron AI, d-Matrix, and DEEPX.

Two triangulation methods anchor the valuation. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $137M. Peer-median valuation across five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comps produces $2.0B, based on a median valuation of $2.0B with a wide dispersion ranging from $529M (DEEPX, Series C) to $11.0B (SambaNova Systems, Series A). The dispersion reflects heterogeneity in investor appetite and technology differentiation within frontier semiconductor infrastructure.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $116M (low, stage-progression floor) to $2.3B (high, peer-median adjusted). The median implies $1.1B. Key uncertainties include product-market validation, capital intensity of further R&D, path to revenue, and whether peer valuations reflect durable moats or speculative froth in AI chip infrastructure. The wide gap between stage-progression and peer-median results—spanning nearly two orders of magnitude—underscores execution risk and the sensitivity of frontier-tech valuations to technical milestones.
